channel.title: Press Event on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Press Event on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Temple Of Elemental Evil Design Highlights
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Temple of Elemental Evil design highlights.

This is a document I found in my archive. I was looking through it for something else, but I thought I’d share this because for all you people out there who are developing games, at some point you’re going to be asked to promote this game, maybe to press or maybe to fans, maybe at a conference or a convention, or maybe in a recorded interview or a playthrough. You should know what is important to talk about at such interviews and events, including what makes your game special or unique or why the press should be interested in it or why a player would want to play it I was asked by Atari to make this list, which they asked me. They said: “Could you make some design highlights”. This was because they were already planning for press events. This is dated May 13th 2003., there was a press event coming up in August, which is just a few months away. I wasn’t going to do that press event. That was going to be somebody else on the team. They had a family emergency. Someone else said they’d do it, but they didn’t have a passport that was valid, so I did it I have a video on this. It’s the Temple of Elemental Evil. Press event Took place in August 2003 in a castle south of London.

item.title: Editors Day Talk, April 2000
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to try to recreate the presentation I gave at editor’s day in April of 2000.

I have a video about it. I’ll link below. We did an Arcanum press event at the Magic Castle in April of 2000, about a year before Arcanum came out.

I found my notes on this. I thought they were lost, but I think I’ve told you I have so many paper backups, so many hard drive backups in different formats, that I finally found my notes.

item.title: The Outer Worlds Launch Party Video
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to share with you something special: The Outer Worlds launch party video.

Let me explain so, when The Outer Worlds launched, we had a big launch party for it on October 25th 2019. It was awesome.

Um, most of obsidian was there, the development team, the publisher came in from uh private division and some of the The Outer Worlds — um, some of the Take 2 folks. We had a lot of other guests.

item.title: Arcanum Press Event
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about arcanum’s press event.

This was a really fun press event that was called editor’s day that we held in April of 2000. Sierra, the publisher, invited press to preview Arcanum at the Magic Castle, which is a club in Los Angeles. It’s a really cool Club. It is only open to magicians and their guests. Professional magicians and some pretty famous people have appeared at the magic castle.

item.title: Fallout 4 Launch Party
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the Fallout 4 launch party.

I had a lot of fun talking about the Fallout 3 a press event and launch party, and I got invited to the Fallout 4 launch party, which was again up in LA. Um, I got an email from Todd Howard while I was at obsidian working on tyranny and he said he’d love to have me at the launch party, and this time I got a ticket with a plus one, so I took Robert up with me. It was the party was on November 5th 2015.. So five days before Fallout 4 shipped. So that was really cool.

item.title: Temple Of Elemental Evil Press Event
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the Temple of Elemental Evil press event.

I had a lot of fun talking a few days ago about going to the Fallout 3 launch party, so I thought I’d talk about this, because this is an actual crooked game. This happened in July of 2003. Atari was doing a press event, a combined preced event, for a lot of different games that they had, and they were doing it in London, which I thought was kind of interesting.

item.title: Fallout 3: Press Event and Launch Party
item.description:
Hey everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout 3 and the E3 showing and the ship party

I got to attend. I was reminded of this when, yesterday, I was talking about not going to Fallout 2’s ship party, and it made me go look up my notes, because I know I went to Fallout 3s and it was an amazing party and I will tell about it. We even got some pictures, but the reason I’m wearing my glasses today is I found my notes and, rather than talk about those two things, I want to read you my notes on them because I want to put you into the headspace I was in. These were written back at the time and so I will start with the dates on them. So, and before I start, let me just say that you know, it was really, really cool that you know I this was started off in 2007, so it’s 10 years after Fallout 1 had shipped. I thought was very cool that I got. Invited to these things, so once again I’m going to thank Todd Howard and Pete Hines for extending this invitation. So first let me start with the Fallout 3 E3 showing.
